description: Escalation ladder for AR collections. days are relative to the invoice due date (negative = before due). The /ar-review skill classifies every open invoice into the highest stage whose trigger has passed and that hasn't been actioned yet (per the collections log).

global_rules.broken_promise: If a logged promise-to-pay date passes without payment, advance immediately to the next stage and flag for a phone call regardless of days_past_due.
global_rules.partial_payment: Restart the clock from the remaining balance's original due date; note the partial in all subsequent touches.
global_rules.dispute: If the customer disputes the invoice, freeze dunning, flag as DISPUTE in the digest, and track resolution separately.
global_rules.small_balance_action: Below the floor, skip stages 3-5; roll into a monthly statement instead.

standard.description: Commercial wholesale accounts (retail stores, cafes, distributors).

stages.name: firm
stages.goal: Obtain a specific promise-to-pay date and log it.

stages.name: escalate
stages.goal: Reach a decision-maker. Email is the paper trail; the call is the collection.

stages.name: final
stages.note: Owner decides: hold future orders, payment plan, or write-off/collections agency.

school_district.description: K-12 districts and other public institutions. Slower by process, not by intent: PO-driven, shared AP inboxes, scheduled check runs, sometimes board approval. Longer fuse, but escalation targets a named human sooner.

stages.name: sent
requirements: submit via the district's required channel
confirm receipt if portal gives no acknowledgment

stages.name: courtesy
stages.note: Ask which check run the invoice is scheduled on — this converts a reminder into a promise-to-pay date.

stages.name: firm
stages.goal: Confirm the invoice is approved and scheduled; if not approved, find out what is blocking it (missing PO match, receiving not confirmed, wrong remit address).

stages.name: escalate
stages.escalate_to: AP supervisor or district business manager
stages.note: Districts almost never refuse to pay; stuck invoices are process failures. The call finds the failure.

stages.name: final
stages.note: Before any hold on a district: owner call. Fall food service is a relationship business.
